status_detail: Engine v4 tRPC JSON envelope is structurally correct (HTTP 200, application/json, 22-field message obj). Response body verbatim: array `[{result:{data:{json:{chatId:'2b953573-a51d-4e50-ac37-6f939d21e473',title:'보안 경고',messages:[{...}]},meta:{values:{...}}}}}]`. SuperJSON meta.values correctly tags chatId/messages.0.id as 'uuid' and createdAt as 'Date'. contentChunks[0].text contains the full Korean warning (not truncated): '⚠️ 민감정보가 포함된 요청은 보안 정책에 의해 차단되었습니다.'. The 'title' field is also '보안 경고'. HOWEVER warning DID NOT render in chat UI -- frontend Le Chat at /chat does not render the assistant message in-place after newChat. It normally relies on client-side router.push to /chat/{chatId} or a follow-up tRPC fetch (message.list / chat.get) to actually display messages. Our intercept returns a syntactically valid envelope so React Query doesn't error, but the navigation/follow-up step never fires.
intercept_path_seen: /api/trpc/message.newChat?batch=1 (POST). APF internal route stays '/' as configured; visible request URL is the real Mistral tRPC endpoint, not narrowed.
errors_observed: No tRPC error in body (no Error 6002, no schema-mismatch error).
Initial page load showed 63 console 403s on _next/static/chunks/*.js (resolved by service-worker cache after F5).
After submit, console grew to 83 errors -- mostly CSP-report-candidate spam, no app-level errors related to message rendering.
root_cause_hypothesis: Envelope shape is structurally valid but DOES NOT match what Le Chat frontend expects. Real Mistral newChat probably returns a top-level 'chat' wrapper (not 'messages' array directly under json), or the frontend immediately follows newChat with a chained call (e.g. message.list?chatId=X or chat.get?chatId=X) that we are NOT intercepting. Our envelope satisfies the parser but the UI rendering pipeline expects a different shape OR a chained-call response.
recommendation_for_dev: R-3 priority A: capture an UNINTERCEPTED real Mistral newChat response to compare envelope shape (likely a 'chat' wrapper or messages stored under chat.messages, not a direct messages array).

R-3 priority B: monitor full Network panel after newChat in unintercepted run -- check for chained tRPC calls (message.list, chat.get, conversation.get) that the frontend invokes BEFORE rendering. These chained calls likely also need intercept.
Consider router behavior: real Mistral may router.push to /chat/{chatId} which triggers a server-rendered fetch -- intercept v4 may need to handle a GET on /api/trpc/chat.get?batch=1&input=... too.
notes: Cycle 95 R-2 -- mistral v4 tRPC batch JSON envelope verify. PARTIAL because envelope is delivered correctly (gold finding: full verbatim body in response_body.txt, 2755 bytes) but UI does not render. Subagent duration 10min exceeded hard timeout 8min, but returned valid PARTIAL verdict with actionable diagnostic. Key finding for dev: the bug is now downstream of intercept -- frontend rendering pipeline / chained tRPC calls.
